selfCheck.title: Check it yourself
selfCheck.body: Copy the prompt and paste it into your AI so it evaluates the offer for you.

shared.heroTrustLine: Check it yourself: copy the prompt and ask Claude, Gemini, or ChatGPT to evaluate miyagisanchez.com and tell you whether it fits.

migrationCallout.title: Coming from another platform?
migrationCallout.body: If you already have your catalog built on Shopify, Tiendanube, WooCommerce, or Big Cartel, bring it to Miyagi Sánchez without re-typing it — a step-by-step guide per platform.

faqs.title: Is it really free?
faqs.body: Yes. 0% commission per sale. We do not charge you to open a shop or to sell.

faqs.title: Then how do you make money?
faqs.body: With ads in our print magazine and optional premium services. We never take your sale.

faqs.title: Do I need technical skills?
faqs.body: No. Your own AI can assemble the shop with you; you review, publish, and sell.

faqs.title: How do I get paid?
faqs.body: Directly to your account: card, MercadoPago, SPEI, or cash, depending on what you enable in your shop.

aiChannel.eyebrow: A channel others do not have
aiChannel.title: Let AI sell for you too
aiChannel.body: More and more people ask their AI assistant to find and buy things for them. Your Miyagi shop speaks that language: it is built on UCP/MCP, the open commerce standard for agents (backed by Google, Stripe, Shopify, Visa, and more). That means Claude, Gemini, ChatGPT, and other agents can find your catalog, recommend it, and buy — a sales channel most platforms still do not have.

steps.title: You publish your normal catalog.
steps.body: Just like any shop.

steps.title: Miyagi exposes it to agents.
steps.body: Your catalog ends up in the open format agents understand, with no extra work from you.

steps.title: An agent discovers it and buys.
steps.body: An AI can find, recommend, and buy your products on behalf of its user.

aiChannel.note: This is not a future promise: the shop is already readable and operable by agents today. Do not believe us? Ask your AI to try it.

metadata.title: Sell with no commissions in Mexico — Miyagi Sanchez
metadata.description: Open a free shop on Miyagi Sanchez. Sell products, services, experiences, or full catalogs with no platform commissions.
metadata.ogAlt: Miyagi Sanchez: sell anything in Mexico with no commissions.

anchor.heroTitle: Sell anything in Mexico. No commissions.
anchor.heroLead: Open your shop in minutes. Join the marketplace or sell on your own domain. Your AI can build it with you — free and low risk.
anchor.trustLine: Do not take our word for it: ask Claude, Gemini, or ChatGPT what miyagisanchez.com is.

heroStats.label: channels: link, subdomain, domain, or widget

heroValues.label: sell on Claude, Gemini, and ChatGPT
premium features included: tickets, sweepstakes, scheduling, and more

anchor.proofTitle: Everything we promise already exists in the platform
anchor.proofLead: No vaporware: publishing, direct payments, owned storefronts, and agents are already part of Miyagi.

proofPoints.title: 0% commission
proofPoints.body: Collect 100%. Your money goes directly to Stripe, MercadoPago, SPEI, or the method you coordinate.

proofPoints.title: Ready in minutes
proofPoints.body: Bring photos, Instagram, text, or a file; your AI can turn it into a publishable shop.

proofPoints.title: Your shop, your brand
proofPoints.body: Use a free link, subdomain, custom domain, or embeddable widget on your site.

proofPoints.title: Built for agents
proofPoints.body: Claude, Gemini, or ChatGPT can read the page, explain the offer, and help you operate.

anchor.routerTitle: What kind of seller are you?
anchor.routerLead: Choose the path that best matches your business. Each route preserves attribution so we can measure which message converts best.

routerCards.title: I am a creator or designer
routerCards.body: Unique pieces, drops, design, fashion, art, or products you sell through DMs today.

routerCards.title: I offer experiences or services
routerCards.body: Tours, rentals, food, transfers, appointments, or services ready for the World Cup.

routerCards.title: I have a local business
routerCards.body: Print shop, workshop, physical store, or inventory you want to bring online.

routerCards.title: I sell professional services
routerCards.body: Consultations, sessions, packages, maintenance, or appointment-based work.

routerCards.title: I sell cars
routerCards.body: Dealer lot, agency, or a private sale — with specs, inspection, and warranty.

steps.title: Ask your AI about us
steps.body: Or go straight in. The page is written so an agent can audit it.

steps.title: Register in seconds
steps.body: Use Google, create your shop, and choose how you want customers to find you.

steps.title: Upload your catalog
steps.body: Paste text, import a file, or let your agent organize it with you.

premiumFeatures.title: All of this is already included
premiumFeatures.lead: Features you pay extra for on other platforms — here they are part of the marketplace.

items.label: Tickets and events
items.sub: Sell admissions and run your event.

items.label: Sweepstakes
items.sub: Sweepstakes campaigns to grow your audience.

items.label: Scheduling and appointments
items.sub: Book with Cal.com for services and experiences.

items.label: Subscriptions and content
items.sub: Charge recurring and sell your own content.

items.label: Coupons and promotions
items.sub: Discounts and codes for your campaigns.

anchor.closingTitle: Start free and leave it if you do not like it.
anchor.closingBody: No contract, no hidden commission, and no need to rebuild your business. Upload a first offer and try it.

b.heroTitle: Open your free shop in Mexico. 0% commission.

benchmark.title: Compare before you decide
benchmark.lead: What you pay on other platforms, you keep here. Compare it yourself — or ask your AI.

rows.label: Commission per sale
rows.shopify: 0% of its own, but +2% if you do not use Shopify Payments

rows.label: Payment processing
rows.miyagi: Your gateway's, straight to your account
rows.mercadoLibre: Included in the commission; paid out later

rows.label: Where does your money land?
rows.miyagi: Straight to your account (Stripe, MercadoPago, SPEI, cash)
rows.mercadoLibre: Held and released by Mercado Libre
rows.shopify: Via Shopify Payments or your gateway

rows.label: Your own shop and domain
rows.miyagi: Yes: link, subdomain, domain, or widget
rows.mercadoLibre: No: you live inside Mercado Libre

benchmark.footnote: Compared against the public rates of Mercado Libre and Shopify Mexico, verified on June 25, 2026. Rates change: ask your AI to confirm the numbers before deciding.

example.title: Example: you sell a $1,000 MXN product
example.lead: How much reaches you, depending on where you sell.
example.punchline: On a $1,000 sale you keep ~$130 more than on Mercado Libre — and with no monthly fee like Shopify.
footnotes: Less your payment processor's fee (Stripe, MercadoPago ≈3.5% + $3), the same on any platform and straight to your account.
Average Clásica commission; varies by category (≈8–16%). Low-priced products pay an extra fixed charge.
With Shopify Payments; +2% if you use another gateway.
Basic plan ≈$19–39 USD/mo + VAT — whether or not you sell.

metadata.title: Sell cars with specs, inspection, and monthly-payment display — Miyagi Sanchez

autos.heroTitle: Sell cars with the trust of a professional dealer lot.

autos.trustLine: Do not take our word for it: ask your AI to evaluate miyagisanchez.com/vende/autos and tell you if it works for your car catalog.

heroStats.label: real filters: make, model, year, price, mileage
informative monthly payment on every listing

autos.proofTitle: Built to sell cars, not just post listings
autos.proofLead: Every car gets specs, real filters, and the trust signals a buyer expects before deciding.

proofPoints.title: Full spec sheet
proofPoints.body: Make, model, year, mileage, transmission, and color in a clear table — your buyer decides with data, not just photos.

proofPoints.title: Search by make, year, and price

proofPoints.title: Inspection report and warranty

steps.title: Share your link

metadata.title: Switch to Miyagi Sánchez from Shopify, Tiendanube, WooCommerce, or Big Cartel
metadata.description: Bring your catalog to Miyagi Sánchez in minutes: a direct connector for Shopify, guided export for Tiendanube, WooCommerce, and Big Cartel. Free to start on your own, or with a consultant if you'd rather someone else did it.
metadata.ogAlt: Switch to Miyagi Sánchez from your current platform.

migracion.heroTitle: Switch to Miyagi Sánchez in minutes, not weeks.
migracion.heroLead: You already have your catalog built on another platform — don't re-type it by hand. Pick where you're coming from and follow the exact steps to bring it to Miyagi Sánchez.
migracion.trustLine: Do not take our word for it: ask your AI to evaluate miyagisanchez.com/vende/migracion and tell you if your catalog can be brought over without re-typing it.
migracion.secondaryCta: I'd rather someone else did it

heroStats.label: up to 500 products, to start on your own

migracion.proofTitle: Three ways to bring your catalog
migracion.proofLead: Pick whichever fits best — all three end up in the same place: your catalog, reviewed by you and published on Miyagi Sánchez.

proofPoints.title: Shopify: direct connector
proofPoints.body: Type in your Shopify domain and Miyagi Sánchez brings your catalog (titles, variants, prices, photos) on its own — no exporting by hand.

proofPoints.title: Tiendanube, WooCommerce, Big Cartel: export and upload
proofPoints.body: Export your catalog from your current platform (CSV or JSON, depending which one you use) and upload it to Miyagi Sánchez's importer — we review it before publishing anything.

proofPoints.title: Don't want to do it yourself? A consultant does it for you
proofPoints.body: A Miyagi Sánchez consultant comes to your business, photographs your catalog, and leaves it set up the same day. Flat price up to a certain catalog size; bigger catalogs get a quote before anything is charged.

migracion.routerTitle: Which platform are you coming from?
migracion.routerLead: Each one has its own guide with the exact export steps.

routerCards.title: Shopify
routerCards.body: Just your domain — Miyagi Sánchez brings your catalog automatically.

routerCards.title: Tiendanube
routerCards.body: Download your product list as a CSV from your dashboard.

routerCards.title: WooCommerce
routerCards.body: Use the product exporter WordPress already has.

routerCards.title: Big Cartel
routerCards.body: Download your public catalog and paste it — your AI organizes it.

migracion.stepsTitle: How it works, no matter where you're coming from

steps.title: 1 · Bring in your catalog
steps.body: With the connector (Shopify) or by uploading your exported file (Tiendanube, WooCommerce, Big Cartel).

steps.title: 2 · Review before publishing
steps.body: Nothing goes live on its own — you review each product (title, price, photos) and confirm.

steps.title: 3 · Publish on Miyagi Sánchez
steps.body: Your catalog lands on your own shop, searchable and with no platform commission.

migracion.socialTitle: For any catalog, big or small
migracion.socialBody: Nothing publishes on its own: you (or your consultant) review every product before it goes on sale.

socialStats.label: you can also paste your catalog and let AI organize it

migracion.closingTitle: Your catalog already exists — you just need to bring it over.
migracion.closingBody: Pick your current platform above and follow the guide. If you'd rather someone else did it, ask for a Miyagi Sánchez consultant.

metadata.title: Migrate your Shopify catalog to Miyagi Sánchez
metadata.description: Bring your Shopify catalog to Miyagi Sánchez with the direct connector: type in your domain and your products, variants, prices, and photos arrive on their own, ready to review.
metadata.ogAlt: Migrate your Shopify catalog to Miyagi Sánchez with the direct connector.

migracionShopify.heroTitle: Your Shopify catalog, without re-typing it.
migracionShopify.heroLead: Type in your Shopify shop's domain and Miyagi Sánchez brings your products — titles, variants, prices, stock, and photos — straight into a review before publishing anything.
migracionShopify.trustLine: Do not take our word for it: ask your AI to review miyagisanchez.com/vende/migracion/shopify and tell you if your Shopify shop can be brought over this way.

heroStats.label: thing you need: your Shopify domain
installs or access you have to grant

migracionShopify.proofTitle: What the connector brings — and what it doesn't
migracionShopify.proofLead: It's honest about its limits: it brings your catalog and your policies as text; your shop's design you build yourself with Miyagi Sánchez's templates.

proofPoints.title: Your full catalog
proofPoints.body: Titles, variants, options, prices, stock, and photos for every product — without re-typing them.

proofPoints.title: Policies and FAQs
proofPoints.body: The text of your shipping policies, returns, and FAQs comes over too, so you can review and adapt it.

proofPoints.title: A report of what maps and what doesn't
proofPoints.body: Before importing anything, you see a clear report: which sections of your shop have an equivalent on Miyagi Sánchez and which don't.

proofPoints.title: Nothing publishes without your confirmation
proofPoints.body: Your catalog arrives as a draft. You decide what to publish and when — nothing goes on sale on its own.

migracionShopify.stepsTitle: The 4 steps, from your domain to your published catalog

steps.title: 1 · Type in your domain
steps.body: Go to your Miyagi Sánchez dashboard → Import catalog → Migrate from Shopify, and type in your domain (e.g. yourshop.com or yourshop.myshopify.com).

steps.title: 2 · Wait about a minute
steps.body: Miyagi Sánchez brings your whole catalog automatically — you don't need to export or upload any file.

steps.title: 3 · Review the parity report
steps.body: See which parts of your shop (banners, theme, collections, pages) have an equivalent on Miyagi Sánchez before deciding.

steps.title: 4 · Choose what to import and publish
steps.body: Select the products you want to bring over. They land as drafts in your catalog — you review and publish them.

migracionShopify.socialTitle: For Shopify shops of any size
migracionShopify.socialBody: Large catalogs can be brought over too — if yours is very custom, a Miyagi Sánchez consultant will contact you directly before charging anything.

socialStats.label: available if you'd rather someone else did it

migracionShopify.closingTitle: Your Shopify catalog could be on Miyagi Sánchez today.
migracionShopify.closingBody: You just need your domain. The rest — variants, prices, photos — arrives on its own, ready for you to review.

metadata.title: Migrate your Tiendanube catalog to Miyagi Sánchez
metadata.description: Export your product list from your Tiendanube dashboard and upload it to Miyagi Sánchez's importer — exact steps, without re-typing your catalog by hand.
metadata.ogAlt: Migrate your Tiendanube catalog to Miyagi Sánchez.

migracionTiendanube.heroTitle: Your Tiendanube catalog, in a file and ready to upload.
migracionTiendanube.heroLead: Tiendanube already has a built-in product exporter. Download your list as a CSV and upload it straight into Miyagi Sánchez's importer — without re-typing a single product.
migracionTiendanube.trustLine: Do not take our word for it: ask your AI to review miyagisanchez.com/vende/migracion/tiendanube and tell you if your Tiendanube export fits Miyagi Sánchez's importer.

heroStats.label: the same file Tiendanube already exports

migracionTiendanube.proofTitle: From your Tiendanube dashboard to Miyagi Sánchez's catalog
migracionTiendanube.proofLead: Tiendanube exports a CSV compatible with Excel and Google Sheets — Miyagi Sánchez's importer recognizes its columns (name, price, category, stock) automatically.

proofPoints.title: Built-in exporter, no extra apps
proofPoints.body: You don't need to install anything — Tiendanube already has a button to export your product list right inside your dashboard.

proofPoints.title: Columns recognized automatically
proofPoints.body: Name, price, category, stock, and description slot in on their own in Miyagi Sánchez's format — you don't have to rename columns.

proofPoints.title: Review before publishing
proofPoints.body: Every imported product lands as a draft in your catalog — you confirm before it's visible to the public.

migracionTiendanube.stepsTitle: The exact steps in your Tiendanube dashboard

steps.title: 1 · Go to Products → Product list
steps.body: From your Tiendanube control panel, go to the products section.

steps.title: 2 · Click "Import and export" → Export
steps.body: Above your product list. Check "Include descriptions" if you want to keep them.

steps.title: 3 · Download the CSV file
steps.body: Tiendanube hands it to you ready to go, compatible with Excel and Google Sheets.

steps.title: 4 · Upload it to Miyagi Sánchez
steps.body: In your Miyagi Sánchez dashboard → Import catalog, drag in the same CSV file. Review the preview and confirm.

migracionTiendanube.socialTitle: Works the same regardless of your Tiendanube plan
migracionTiendanube.socialBody: Product export isn't available on Tiendanube's free Inicial plan — if that's you, you can copy and paste your catalog straight into Miyagi Sánchez's importer and let the AI organize it.

socialStats.label: or copy-paste if your plan can't export

migracionTiendanube.closingTitle: Your Tiendanube catalog is already ready to export.
migracionTiendanube.closingBody: Download your CSV from your dashboard and upload it to Miyagi Sánchez's importer — most columns recognize themselves.

metadata.title: Migrate your WooCommerce catalog to Miyagi Sánchez
metadata.description: Export your products from WooCommerce (Products → All Products → Export) and upload them to Miyagi Sánchez's importer — exact steps, without re-typing your catalog.
metadata.ogAlt: Migrate your WooCommerce catalog to Miyagi Sánchez.

migracionWoocommerce.heroTitle: Your WooCommerce catalog, exported in one click.
migracionWoocommerce.heroLead: WordPress has had a built-in product exporter in WooCommerce for years. Download your CSV and upload it to Miyagi Sánchez's importer — no plugins, no extra steps.
migracionWoocommerce.trustLine: Do not take our word for it: ask your AI to review miyagisanchez.com/vende/migracion/woocommerce and tell you if your WooCommerce export fits Miyagi Sánchez's importer.

heroStats.label: with the exporter WooCommerce already has

migracionWoocommerce.proofTitle: From your WordPress to Miyagi Sánchez's catalog
migracionWoocommerce.proofLead: WooCommerce's exporter has been built-in since version 3.1 — you don't need any extra plugin to get your catalog out.

proofPoints.title: Exporter built into WooCommerce
proofPoints.body: It's under Products → All Products → Export, with nothing else to install.

proofPoints.title: Variations included
proofPoints.body: WooCommerce's exporter includes product variations (size, color, etc.) — Miyagi Sánchez's importer receives them too.

proofPoints.title: Review before publishing
proofPoints.body: Every imported product lands as a draft in your catalog — you confirm before it's visible to the public.

migracionWoocommerce.stepsTitle: The exact steps in your WordPress

steps.title: 1 · Go to Products → All Products
steps.body: From your WordPress admin dashboard.

steps.title: 2 · Click "Export"
steps.body: Above your product list. Leave "Export all columns" checked so no data gets left out.

steps.title: 3 · Download the CSV file
steps.body: WooCommerce generates it instantly.

steps.title: 4 · Upload it to Miyagi Sánchez
steps.body: In your Miyagi Sánchez dashboard → Import catalog, drag in the file. Miyagi Sánchez recognizes WooCommerce's name, price, category, and image columns automatically; review the preview and confirm.

migracionWoocommerce.socialBody: If your catalog is too large for one file, split it into parts — Miyagi Sánchez's importer accepts several uploads in a row without duplicating products.
migracionWoocommerce.closingTitle: Your WooCommerce catalog is already ready to export.
migracionWoocommerce.closingBody: Use the exporter WordPress already has and upload it to Miyagi Sánchez — no plugins, no extra steps.

metadata.title: Migrate your Big Cartel catalog to Miyagi Sánchez
metadata.description: Big Cartel has no export button in its dashboard — here's exactly how to pull your catalog and bring it to Miyagi Sánchez with the help of AI.
metadata.ogAlt: Migrate your Big Cartel catalog to Miyagi Sánchez.

migracionBigcartel.heroTitle: Your Big Cartel catalog, with no export button — but it can be done.
migracionBigcartel.heroLead: Big Cartel doesn't have an "export" option in its dashboard. Here's the honest path: your public catalog CAN be downloaded, and your AI can organize it for you in Miyagi Sánchez's importer.
migracionBigcartel.trustLine: Do not take our word for it: ask your AI to review miyagisanchez.com/vende/migracion/bigcartel and tell you if your public Big Cartel catalog can be brought over this way.

heroStats.label: export button in Big Cartel's dashboard
organizes your catalog for you, no column mapping

migracionBigcartel.proofTitle: The honest path for Big Cartel
migracionBigcartel.proofLead: Unlike Tiendanube or WooCommerce, Big Cartel doesn't offer a ready-to-import CSV — so the path here is different, and we tell you plainly.

proofPoints.title: No official CSV
proofPoints.body: Big Cartel doesn't have an "export products" button like Tiendanube or WooCommerce — its catalog downloads from a public page instead.

proofPoints.title: Your public catalog, in a file
proofPoints.body: Your own shop's yourdomain.com/products.json address hands over your full catalog (titles, variants, prices, photos) — no password needed.

proofPoints.title: Paste it and let AI organize it
proofPoints.body: Instead of uploading that file as a CSV (its columns don't match a normal CSV's), paste it into the "Paste catalog" option in Miyagi Sánchez's importer — the AI arranges it for you.

migracionBigcartel.stepsTitle: The exact steps for Big Cartel

steps.title: 1 · Open your public catalog in JSON
steps.body: In your browser, go to yourdomain.com/products.json (use your actual Big Cartel shop domain).

steps.title: 2 · Copy all the content
steps.body: Select all the text on that page (Ctrl/Cmd+A, Ctrl/Cmd+C) or save it as a text file.

steps.title: 3 · Paste it into Miyagi Sánchez's importer
steps.body: In your Miyagi Sánchez dashboard → Import catalog, use the paste-text option (not the CSV upload) and paste the content.

steps.title: 4 · Review what the AI organized
steps.body: The AI splits out each product with its title, price, and photos. Review, fix anything that needs it, and confirm before publishing.

migracionBigcartel.socialTitle: For Big Cartel shops of any size
migracionBigcartel.socialBody: If your catalog is large or has many variants, a Miyagi Sánchez consultant can run this process for you — ask for one from the migration guide.
migracionBigcartel.closingTitle: No export button, but your catalog can still be brought over.
migracionBigcartel.closingBody: Copy your public catalog as JSON and paste it into Miyagi Sánchez's importer — the AI does the work of organizing it.

metadata.title: Promoter Program — Miyagi Sánchez
metadata.description: Resources for promoters: set up the merchant's shop, close the sale in person, and hand off over WhatsApp. Glossary, pricing, and a step-by-step guide.
metadata.ogAlt: Miyagi Sánchez Promoter Program: set up, close, hand off.

promotor.heroTitle: Set up the shop, close in person, hand off over WhatsApp.
promotor.heroLead: You're Miyagi Sánchez's field force. Walk into the business, leave it set up on miyagisanchez.com, collect cash in person, and earn your commission. Here's everything to explain and sell it.
promotor.trustLine: Commission on every closed sale. Settlement by transfer or cash.
promotor.proofTitle: Glossary: what you sell the merchant
promotor.proofLead: Four products. Learn them to explain each in 30 seconds.

glossary.title: Own domain
glossary.body: Their shop on their own address (theirshop.mx) instead of miyagisanchez.com/s/theirshop. More brand, direct traffic, white-label look. $499 MXN per year, one-time.

glossary.title: Subdomain
glossary.body: theirshop.miyagisanchez.com — free for everyone. Use it as a hook: a nice address at no cost.

glossary.title: Printed ad
glossary.body: A spot in Miyagi Sánchez's printed edition circulating in their area. Price depends on size and edition; charged once.

glossary.title: Mercado Libre sync
glossary.body: Import their Mercado Libre catalog into Miyagi Sánchez so they don't re-enter everything by hand. Speeds up the first-visit setup.

glossary.title: Store migration
glossary.body: Photograph their catalog (or bring their Shopify/Tiendanube/WooCommerce/Big Cartel export) and leave it set up the same day. Flat price up to a catalog size limit — bigger catalogs get a quote before you charge anything. The exact price is in your migration handbook.

promotor.stepsTitle: How to set up and hand off (in the shop)

steps.title: 1 · Set up the shop
steps.body: From your workspace, create the merchant's shop (it stays unclaimed) and add a first listing. Show them how easy it is.

steps.title: 2 · Collect and pay
steps.body: Collect cash from the merchant and pay for the domain or ad with your own card. The sale is attributed to your code and the entitlement lands on THEIR shop.

steps.title: 3 · Hand off over WhatsApp
steps.body: Generate the claim link and send it over WhatsApp. They tap it, sign in, and the shop transfers to their account. Your commission survives.

promotor.pitchBody: Your promoter code (PRM-…) gives the merchant a discount on the domain or ad. It's your best closing argument: a price only you can give them, today, in person.

apply.title: Apply to become a promoter
apply.body: Tell us who you are. We'll review your application and get back to you in 2–3 business days.

form.motivationLabel: Why do you want to be a promoter? (optional)
form.success: Done! We received your application — we'll email you in 2–3 business days.
form.genericError: Couldn't send the application. Please try again.

promotor.handbookLead: The full close checklist, start to finish, so you never have to ask Daniel anything.

checklist.title: 1 · Set up
checklist.body: Create the merchant's shop from your workspace (it stays unclaimed) and add a first listing.

checklist.title: 2 · Collect
checklist.body: Collect cash from the merchant and pay for the domain, subdomain, or ad with your own card. The sale is attributed to your code.

checklist.title: 3 · Design the ad (when it applies)
checklist.body: If the close includes a printed ad: designing it from your workspace is coming soon — for now, coordinate the design directly with the team.

checklist.title: 4 · Hand off over WhatsApp
checklist.body: Generate the claim link and send it over WhatsApp. They tap it, sign in, and the shop transfers to their account.

checklist.title: 5 · Send the receipt
checklist.body: The automatic receipt to the merchant is coming soon — for now, let them know over WhatsApp yourself that everything is activated.

scripts.title: Own domain
scripts.body: "Did you know your shop could live at yourname.mx instead of a long link? Looks more professional and people find you directly. It's $499 a year, one-time, and with my code I can activate it today."

scripts.title: Subdomain
scripts.body: "Your shop can have its own address, yourname.miyagisanchez.com, without buying a domain. It's $199 a year, but with my code it's FREE the first year — I can activate it today."

scripts.title: Printed ad
scripts.body: "We run in print and circulate in your area — people who don't even search online will find you there. Price depends on size, charged once, and with my code you get a price you can't get on your own."

scripts.title: Mercado Libre sync
scripts.body: "If you already sell on Mercado Libre you don't have to re-enter anything — we connect your catalog and it's all here too, same day."

scripts.title: Store migration
scripts.body: "Coming from Shopify, Tiendanube, WooCommerce, or Big Cartel? I'll get you set up today — I photograph your catalog or bring your export, and in one visit it's all on your own Miyagi Sánchez shop. Flat price up to a catalog size limit; if yours is bigger, I'll give you a quote before charging anything."

promotor.paymentsBody: You pay for the domain, subdomain, or ad with your own card when you set up the shop. The merchant pays you back in cash (or however you agree). Your commission isn't charged separately: Miyagi Sánchez records it on your account and the team settles it with you afterward, by transfer or cash.
promotor.paymentsComingSoon: Coming soon: you'll be able to collect the full payment and transfer only what's owed, keeping your commission on the spot — no waiting for settlement.
promotor.closingBody: Open your workspace, bind your code, and start setting up shops. Every closed sale is commission for you.

promotorMigracion.title: Migration handbook
promotorMigracion.lead: The step-by-step of an in-person migration, start to finish — what to photograph, what to ask, and when to charge flat price, a quote, or hand the case to Daniel.

photo.title: Their current catalog
photo.body: Screenshots of their product list on their current platform (name, price, photo) — a backup in case anything doesn't import cleanly.

photo.title: The total product count
photo.body: How many products they have in total — decides whether the close is flat price or needs a quote (see below).

photo.title: Their logo and colors
photo.body: If they have a defined brand (logo, colors on their current shop), photograph them to give their Miyagi Sánchez shop the same look.

photo.title: Shipping and returns policies
photo.body: Photograph or copy the text wherever they have them written — it transcribes directly into their Miyagi Sánchez shop.

interview.title: Which platform are you coming from?
interview.body: Shopify uses the direct connector (just the domain); Tiendanube, WooCommerce, and Big Cartel export a file first.

interview.title: How do you get paid today?
interview.body: Card, transfer, cash — so you can activate the same payment methods on their new Miyagi Sánchez shop from day one.

interview.title: How do you ship?
interview.body: To configure the same (or closest) shipping method on their new shop.

interview.title: Is there anything very custom about your current shop?
interview.body: Themes, sections, or pages beyond catalog, shipping, and basic policies — if there are, that's information for the parity report, not something you promise to replicate yourself.

run.title: 1 · Bring in the catalog
run.body: Shopify: use the connector with their domain (Workspace → Import catalog → Migrate from Shopify). Tiendanube/WooCommerce/Big Cartel: ask for their export (or help them generate it right there with the /vende/migracion guides) and upload it at Workspace → Import catalog.

run.title: 2 · Review the parity report
run.body: If it's coming from Shopify, review the report of what maps and what doesn't before continuing — it decides whether the case is flat price, a quote, or "very custom."

run.title: 3 · Decide the price (see below) and close
run.body: From /promotor/cerrar, choose "Store migration" and charge accordingly.

run.title: 4 · Review and publish with the merchant
run.body: Go through the imported catalog together, fix anything that needs it, and publish in front of them — so they see their shop working.

promotorMigracion.decisionTitle: Flat price, quote, or Daniel — how to decide

decision.title: Flat price
decision.body: Catalog within the flat-price migration limit (check the exact number above, next to the price) and nothing out of the ordinary. Close directly from /promotor/cerrar with no quote ID.

decision.title: Quote
decision.body: Catalog bigger than the flat-price limit. From the parity report (only available for Shopify migrations for now), generate the quote, copy the ID for the merchant, and paste it into /promotor/cerrar when charging — the amount charged is always exactly the quote's, never a different number.

decision.title: Hand the case to Daniel
decision.body: If the report flags the shop as "very custom" (an incomplete pull or an out-of-the-ordinary catalog), no price is generated at all — neither flat nor quoted. Let the merchant know Daniel will contact them directly, and don't promise or charge any amount yourself.

metadata.title: Sell World Cup experiences — Miyagi Sanchez
metadata.description: Publish tours, food spots, and rentals for the 2026 World Cup in Mexico. No platform commissions and payouts to your own account.
metadata.ogAlt: Miyagi Sanchez World Cup 2026: sell experiences in Mexico with no commissions.

mundial.heroTitle: Capture the World Cup's global audience.
mundial.heroLead: Publish your tours, food spots, and rentals instantly, with no platform commissions. Get ready before demand reaches Mexico City, Guadalajara, and Monterrey.
mundial.trustLine: Do not take our word for it: ask Claude about miyagisanchez.com/vende/mundial and let it audit the page for you.
mundial.demandTitle: What visitors are already looking for
mundial.proofTitle: Built for services and rentals, not only products
mundial.proofLead: We reuse Miyagi's current infrastructure: publishing, payments, scheduling, and agent access. No new backend for this page.

proofPoints.title: Services and rentals
proofPoints.body: The onboarding already supports service and rental listings with location, price, or price-on-request.

proofPoints.title: 0% commission
proofPoints.body: Miyagi charges no platform commission. Your margin stays with you.

proofPoints.title: Payouts to your account
proofPoints.body: Turn on Stripe Connect, MercadoPago, or direct-payment coordination depending on how your shop operates.

proofPoints.title: Scheduling when needed
proofPoints.body: Connect Cal.com from your shop for experiences with time slots, capacity, or appointments.

steps.title: Create your shop
steps.body: Name, location, and basics so visitors know who is helping them.

steps.title: Upload your experience
steps.body: Describe the tour, rental, or service with price, area, photos, and conditions.

steps.title: Share and measure
steps.body: Use your link in social, QR, ads, or messages; Clarity records the visit.

mundial.closingTitle: The World Cup will not wait. Neither should your listing.
mundial.closingBody: If you sell experiences, food, transfers, tours, short-term rentals, or local services, start with a service listing and tune it later.

b.heroTitle: Let the World Cup find your local experience.

terms.title: Terms of use
terms.subtitle: Basic rules for using Miyagi Sanchez.

sections.title: Commission-free marketplace
sections.body: Miyagi Sanchez connects buyers and sellers. Each shop defines its products, prices, payment methods, delivery options, promotions, and policies. When you buy from a shop, the primary commercial relationship is between buyer and seller.

sections.title: Sweepstakes and promotions
sections.body: Each sweepstakes campaign is organized by the shop that publishes it. The shop is responsible for permits, notices, official rules, compliance with SEGOB or any applicable authority, taxes, prize fulfillment, and participant support. Miyagi may pause, hide, or cancel campaigns if there is legal risk, abuse, fraud, complaints, or breach of these rules.

sections.title: Payments and delivery
sections.body: Marketplace product payments flow to the selling shop according to the selected payment method. The seller is responsible for preparing and delivering the product or service unless a specific policy says otherwise.

sections.title: Responsible use
sections.body: Do not use Miyagi for fraud, spam, impersonation, prohibited items, misleading promotions, or illegal activity. We may limit features or suspend accounts and campaigns to protect users and shops.

seller.title: Sweepstakes
seller.subtitle: Create campaigns with verified entry, QR sharing, and automated drawing.
seller.legalNote: Your shop organizes the sweepstakes. Publishing requires permit/reference data, official rules in Spanish and English, and responsibility attestation. SEGOB guidance expects at least 20 business days' notice where applicable.
seller.empty: You do not have any campaigns yet.
seller.attestation: I accept that my shop organizes the sweepstakes and is responsible for legal compliance, prize fulfillment, taxes, and participant communication.

hero.heading: What your neighborhood sells, buys, and recommends

emptyState.heading: The marketplace is taking shape
emptyState.body: The first listings will appear here soon.
emptyState.secondaryCta: Take a walk through the neighborhood

terminalCta.heading: Join the community
terminalCta.body: Save favorites, make offers, and open your shop — no commissions.

page.title: What miyagisanchez.com is — and why sell here
page.lead: An agent-native marketplace for Mexico. Don’t take our word for it — ask your own AI.
page.metaTitle: About miyagisanchez.com — what it is and why sell here
page.metaDescription: What miyagisanchez.com is, why sell, how to start, and what it costs. An agent-native marketplace for Mexico. 0% commission.

what_is.heading: What is miyagisanchez.com?
what_is.lead: A marketplace for Mexico where anyone can sell — built so your own AI assistant can understand it, browse it, and set it up for you.
body: miyagisanchez.com is a multi-seller, peer-to-peer (P2P) marketplace for Mexico. Any person or business opens a shop, lists what they sell, and reaches buyers across several channels, with no cost to start.
It is built on Medusa (the commerce engine) and is agent-native: it follows the open UCP and MCP standards, so an AI assistant can browse the catalog, negotiate, and buy without needing a browser.

points.title: Multi-seller
points.body: Many independent shops on a single platform.

points.title: Built for Mexico
points.body: Local payments and methods: SPEI, cash, DiMo, MercadoPago, and Stripe.

points.title: Agent-native
points.body: Your AI can read us and act on your behalf via UCP and MCP.

why_sell.heading: Why sell here?
why_sell.lead: No commission, on every channel, with your own AI assistant doing the heavy lifting.
body: Selling is free: we charge 0% commission per sale. You keep what you sell.
Your catalog lives once and appears in many places: the marketplace, your own branded domain, a widget you paste on any site, and an API/agent for programmatic purchases.

points.title: 0% commission
points.body: We don’t charge per sale. Listing and selling are free.

points.title: Multi-channel
points.body: Marketplace, own domain, embeddable widget, and API/agents — one catalog.

points.title: AI commerce
points.body: Buyers and agents can browse and negotiate offers natively.

points.title: Express migration
points.body: Import your catalog from a file, pasted text, or JSON; the AI structures it for you.

how_to_start.heading: How to start
how_to_start.lead: From zero to a stocked shop in minutes — and your agent can do almost all of it.
body: Create your account (about 20 seconds with Google) and enter onboarding. Import your catalog by uploading a file, pasting text, or loading a JSON: the AI parses it and creates your products. You review everything in a preview before anything is created, and a step-by-step guide helps you set up how you get paid.
Have an AI assistant? Ask it to read miyagisanchez.com and prepare your shop: the brand dressing and products ready to import. You review and publish.

points.title: 2. Import
points.body: File, pasted text, or JSON → the AI structures your catalog.

points.title: 3. Publish
points.body: Review, tweak, and open your shop.

cost_transparency.heading: How (and why) we keep costs low
cost_transparency.lead: You and your agent do the creative work; we run thin rails.
body: Sellers’ AI assistants do much of the work: cataloging, copywriting, pricing, and maintaining the shop. We provide the rails.
We build on Medusa primitives and reuse before rebuilding, with agent surfaces (UCP/MCP) instead of bespoke tooling. That keeps our infrastructure cost low — and that is why selling can be free.

pricing.heading: Pricing
pricing.lead: Selling is free (0% commission). The optional premium services are a custom subdomain and a custom domain.
body: Your shop is always free: your URL at miyagisanchez.com/s/your-shop costs nothing and never expires, with 0% commission.

Custom subdomain: $199 MXN/year (~$17/mo) or $25 MXN/mo. Your shop as a standalone site at your-shop.miyagisanchez.com (no platform bar). The yearly plan is cheaper; the monthly one has no commitment. You can switch between monthly and yearly anytime with no double charge. It renews automatically; cancel anytime and your shop stays live and free at your URL miyagisanchez.com/s/your-shop.

Custom domain: $499 MXN/year (~$42/mo). Connect your own domain (yourshop.com) with SSL and our infrastructure, with no miyagisanchez.com in the URL. It renews yearly; you can cancel anytime and your shop stays live on your free URL.

founder.heading: Who is behind this
founder.lead: Miyagi Sánchez was built by one person, with real experience running large-scale commerce and building businesses from zero.
body: Daniel Vásquez is the founder. Before this project he led product globally at AB InBev, the largest consumer-goods corporation in the world, directing a team of more than 70 people and capital budgets exceeding $70M USD. Before that, as a senior product manager at Linio, he led the technical integration when Falabella acquired the company.

He has also been a founder twice. At Bicimensajero.com he built the entire software architecture of an on-demand logistics service himself, from scratch, and went on to operate a network of more than 100 bike messengers before negotiating its sale. He holds a Master’s in Finance — the reason this project is designed to run on minimal costs, which is what makes a genuine 0% commission possible.
Want to talk about the project? Write to daniel@miyagisanchez.com.

points.title: 70+ people
points.body: Product and engineering team led at AB InBev.

points.title: $70M+ USD
points.body: Capital budget managed at AB InBev.

points.title: 2 startups founded
points.body: Built and operated from zero: Bicimensajero.com and Pokapok.mx.

philosophy.heading: Philosophy
philosophy.lead: Bring ecommerce best practices to everyone — free, and at the highest level.
body: We believe the AI assistant is the distribution channel and the onboarding engine. Instead of asking you to trust us, we tell you to ask your own Claude, Gemini, or ChatGPT about miyagisanchez.com: your agent reads us, explains it in your terms, and — if it makes sense — sets up your shop.

We provide the rails; you focus on the creative and the strategy. The mission is to democratize commerce best practices — trust, offer negotiation, and AI-native shopping — so anyone can sell at the quality of the giants, at no cost.
